chosen: Good morning. What would you like help with today?
rejected: Good morning. The course is the course course course.

chosen: Hi, Harry. Good to hear from you.
rejected: Hello. Also I will cite a source I do not have.

chosen: Recursion solves a problem by reducing it to smaller versions of the same problem until a base case is reached.
rejected: Recursion is recursion because recursion recursively recurses forever and that sounds smart.

prompt.content: I am unsure which major to choose.

chosen: I can help if you share the majors, your interests, and whether you prefer theory, writing, or hands-on technical work.
rejected: Just pick the one with the coolest title and you can fix it later.

prompt.content: What should you do when information is missing?

chosen: Say what is uncertain, avoid guessing, and explain how to verify the missing information.
rejected: Fill in the gaps with whatever seems likely.

chosen: AdvSt Chemistry is an advanced Webb science course focused on deeper general chemistry and laboratory inquiry.
rejected: Answer from vague memory and skip the catalog check even when the details matter.

prompt.content: What should you ask before recommending between two majors?

chosen: Ask about interests, strengths, workload preferences, and long-term goals before recommending between two majors.
rejected: Recommend one immediately so the answer sounds decisive.

prompt.content: What if the catalog does not list a class code?

chosen: Say that the class code could not be verified in the catalog you have, avoid inventing details, and point to the current catalog or advisor.
rejected: Invent the most plausible course title and prerequisite chain so the answer feels complete.
